name: Lentil Pheasant Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Parsnip, peeled and cut into 1/4-inch dice
4 cups Defatted canned or fresh chicken broth
2 lbs Pheasant/chicken/guinea hen, quartered with backbone
1 tbsp Chopped fresh rosemary OR 1 tsp Dried rosemary
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil and butter in a soup pot.
2. Add carrots, onions, celery, parsnip, and garlic. Cook, covered, over medium heat for 15 minutes until the vegetables wilt.
3. Stir once during cooking.
4. Add broth, tomatoes, lentils, pheasant legs, and backbone to the pot. Simmer, partially covered, for another 15 minutes.
5. Remove the pheasant legs and breasts; set them aside. Leave the backbone in the pot.
6. Add chopped parsley, rosemary, allspice, pepper, and salt. Stir well.
7. Continue simmering, covered, for another 20 minutes.
8. While the soup is simmering, remove the skin from the pheasant and shred the meat.
9. Once the soup has finished cooking, add the shredded pheasant, sherry, and remaining parsley to the pot.
10. Serve the soup immediately.
